summary: The full Question 1.1 — whether $d_H(n)\to 0$ for every fixed graph $H$ with an even number of edges — remains open for general H-codes. Versteegen (arXiv:2310.19891, 2023) proved the analogous statement for the linear variant: if $H$ has an even number of edges then the density of a linear graph code avoiding $H$ is $O(2^{\binom{n}{2}}/\log n)$, and for almost all such $H$ it is at most $2^{\binom{n}{2}/n^{\varepsilon_H}}$ for some $\varepsilon_H>0$. This constitutes partial progress on Alon's question but does not settle it for non-linear codes.

since_posted.title: Upper bounds for linear graph codes
since_posted.claim: Proves that linear H-codes with H having an even number of edges have density O(1/log n), and for almost all such H an exponentially smaller density bound; explicitly described as progress on Alon's question.

search_queries: Alon "graph-codes" "H-code" "even number of edges" symmetric difference 2023
arxiv "graph codes" "H-code" "even number of edges" "d_H(n)" tends to zero 2024 2025
arxiv "upper bounds for linear graph codes" OR "structured codes of graphs" Alon 2023 2024
notes: Versteegen's partial result is for the linear subproblem (codes closed under symmetric difference); it does not imply the general case since linear codes form a strict subset of all H-codes. The paper was published in Random Structures & Algorithms (Wiley) in 2025. The pre-existing 'Structured Codes of Graphs' by Alon and Gujgiczer (arXiv:2202.06810, SIAM J. Discrete Math. 2023) predates the graph-codes paper and is not a follow-up. The full non-linear question appears to remain open as of May 2026.
